note: Iteration 2 - harder, less-common tasks intended to discriminate skill value (deep attribute/extension/header knowledge).

evals.name: hx-sync-abort
evals.prompt: On a form (hx-post="/save") I have a text input that validates itself with hx-post="/validate" on the change event. If the user edits the field and immediately hits Submit, both requests fire and race each other. I want the input's in-flight validation request to be cancelled whenever the form submits, declaratively in htmx with no custom JavaScript. How do I wire this up?
evals.expected_output: Use hx-sync on the input set to coordinate with the enclosing form, e.g. hx-sync="closest form:abort", so the input's request is aborted when the form's request starts/exists.
expectations: Recommends the hx-sync attribute as the declarative mechanism
Syncs against the enclosing form (e.g. selector 'closest form')
Uses the abort strategy (hx-sync="closest form:abort") so the input's request is aborted when the form request is present/starts

evals.name: history-caching-vary
evals.prompt: My Flask app renders full pages on normal requests, but when the HX-Request header is present it returns only a partial fragment. Nav links use hx-get with hx-push-url="true". Two bugs: (1) when a user copies a pushed URL into a new tab, or hits Back after the history cache has expired, they sometimes get a bare partial instead of the full page; (2) a CDN/browser sometimes serves the cached partial fragment for a normal full-page visit to the same URL. How do I make history restoration and caching correct?
evals.expected_output: Pushed URLs must return a full page on direct/restore requests; htmx marks restore requests (HX-History-Restore-Request) and you should distinguish full vs partial server-side, ideally disabling htmx.config.historyRestoreAsHxRequest so HX-Request reliably signals 'return a partial'. Add Vary: HX-Request so caches don't mix partial and full responses for the same URL (or use getCacheBusterParam).
expectations: States that a pushed (hx-push-url) URL must be able to return the FULL page when visited directly or restored
Recommends the 'Vary: HX-Request' response header (or getCacheBusterParam) to stop caches mixing partial and full responses for the same URL
Uses request headers server-side to distinguish request types (HX-Request and/or HX-History-Restore-Request)
References history-restore handling correctly (e.g. htmx.config.historyRestoreAsHxRequest, or HX-History-Restore-Request) so restore requests get the full page

evals.name: sse-live-feed
evals.prompt: I want a live activity feed in htmx 2: my server pushes events over Server-Sent Events at the endpoint /events, and each 'message' event should append a new <li> to a list with id="feed" (items should accumulate, not replace). How do I set this up with htmx?
evals.expected_output: Use the htmx SSE extension: load htmx-ext-sse, enable with hx-ext="sse", open the stream with sse-connect="/events", select the event with sse-swap="message", and append with hx-swap="beforeend".
expectations: Uses the htmx SSE extension (enables hx-ext="sse" AND loads the sse extension script/package, not just core htmx)
Opens the stream with sse-connect="/events" (correct htmx 2 SSE attribute, not the old hx-sse syntax)
Selects the event to swap with sse-swap="message"
Appends items with hx-swap="beforeend" (or equivalent) so the list accumulates instead of being replaced
